hero.title: EU AI Act Compliance Toolkit
hero.subtitle: Open-source tools for navigating Regulation (EU) 2024/1689
hero.description: Classify your AI system's risk tier, track compliance obligations, generate documentation, and stay ahead of enforcement deadlines.

classifier.title: Risk Classification
classifier.description: Interactive wizard to determine whether your AI system is prohibited, high-risk, GPAI, limited, or minimal risk under the EU AI Act.

checklists.title: Compliance Checklists
checklists.description: Tier-specific checklists with article references, evidence tracking, and exportable compliance reports.

timeline.title: Enforcement Timeline
timeline.description: Visual timeline of all EU AI Act enforcement dates — see what's in effect, upcoming, and future.

templates.title: Documentation Templates
templates.description: Generate conformity declarations, risk assessments, model cards, and transparency notices from pre-built templates.

examples.title: Worked Examples
examples.description: Real-world scenarios showing how the classification and compliance process works for different AI systems.

penalties.title: Penalty Calculator
penalties.description: Calculate maximum fine exposure based on your risk tier, organization type, and annual turnover under Article 99.

gaps.title: Gap Analysis
gaps.description: Identify compliance gaps, prioritize remediation actions, and track readiness against enforcement deadlines.

standards.title: Standards Mapping
standards.description: Browse ISO, CEN/CENELEC, and other harmonised standards mapped to EU AI Act requirements.

reports.title: Compliance Reports
reports.description: Generate comprehensive compliance reports combining classification, checklists, gap analysis, and penalty exposure.

classifier.title: AI System Risk Classifier
classifier.subtitle: Determine which EU AI Act risk tier applies to your AI system

prohibited.title: Prohibited Practices
prohibited.description: Check whether your AI system falls under Article 5 prohibited practices

gpai.title: General-Purpose AI
gpai.description: Determine if your system qualifies as a GPAI model

gpaiSystemic.title: Systemic Risk Assessment
gpaiSystemic.description: Assess whether your GPAI model poses systemic risk

highRisk.title: High-Risk Classification
highRisk.description: Check Annex I safety components and Annex III use cases

limited.title: Limited Risk (Transparency)
limited.description: Check transparency obligations for your AI system

prohibited.title: Prohibited AI System
prohibited.description: Your AI system is prohibited under Article 5 of the EU AI Act.
prohibited.action: Discontinue development or fundamentally redesign to remove prohibited capabilities.

highRisk.title: High-Risk AI System
highRisk.description: Your AI system is classified as high-risk. Extensive compliance obligations apply.
highRisk.action: Complete the high-risk compliance checklist and prepare technical documentation.

gpai.title: General-Purpose AI Model
gpai.description: Your model is classified as GPAI. Transparency and documentation obligations apply.
gpai.action: Complete the GPAI compliance checklist including model cards and training data documentation.

gpaiSystemic.title: GPAI with Systemic Risk
gpaiSystemic.description: Your model has systemic risk. Enhanced obligations apply.
gpaiSystemic.action: Complete the GPAI systemic risk checklist including adversarial testing and incident monitoring.

limited.title: Limited Risk AI System
limited.description: Transparency obligations apply. Users must be informed about AI interaction.
limited.action: Implement transparency measures: disclose AI interaction, label synthetic content.

minimal.title: Minimal Risk AI System
minimal.description: No mandatory obligations beyond existing law. Voluntary codes of conduct encouraged.
minimal.action: Consider adopting voluntary codes of conduct for trustworthy AI.

checklists.title: Compliance Checklists
checklists.subtitle: Track your EU AI Act compliance obligations

timeline.title: Enforcement Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Key dates for EU AI Act compliance

templates.title: Documentation Templates
templates.subtitle: Generate compliance documentation from pre-built templates

conformity.title: EU Declaration of Conformity
conformity.description: Required declaration for high-risk AI systems per Article 47 and Annex V.

riskAssessment.title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
riskAssessment.description: Required assessment for high-risk AI systems deployed by public bodies per Article 27.

modelCard.title: GPAI Model Card
modelCard.description: Technical documentation for GPAI models per Article 53 and Annex XI.

impactAssessment.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
impactAssessment.description: DPIA template for AI systems processing personal data, aligned with GDPR Article 35.

transparencyNotice.title: Transparency Notice
transparencyNotice.description: User-facing notice for AI systems with transparency obligations per Article 50.

examples.title: Worked Examples
examples.subtitle: See how the EU AI Act applies to real-world AI systems

footer.description: Open-source EU AI Act compliance toolkit. Built for developers, compliance teams, and legal professionals.
footer.copyright: EU AI Act Toolkit. Open source under MIT License.
footer.regulation: Based on Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council.
footer.tagline: Made with care for AI compliance teams worldwide

disclaimer.short: This tool does not constitute legal advice.
disclaimer.full: This tool does not constitute legal advice. Consult qualified legal counsel for compliance decisions. The EU AI Act Toolkit is an open-source project and is not affiliated with the European Union or any EU institution.

articles.title: EU AI Act Articles
articles.subtitle: Browse and search all articles of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689

penalties.title: Penalty Exposure
penalties.subtitle: Understand the financial consequences of non-compliance with the EU AI Act

calculator.title: Penalty Calculator
calculator.description: Calculate your organization's maximum penalty exposure based on risk tier, organization type, and annual turnover.

overview.title: EU AI Act Penalty Tiers
overview.description: All penalty categories defined in Article 99 of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689.

gaps.title: Compliance Gap Analysis
gaps.subtitle: Identify and prioritize compliance gaps for your AI system

standards.title: Harmonised Standards
standards.subtitle: ISO, CEN/CENELEC, and other standards mapped to EU AI Act requirements

reports.title: Compliance Report Generator
reports.subtitle: Generate a comprehensive compliance report combining classification, checklists, penalties, and gap analysis

form.title: Report Configuration
form.description: Provide details about your AI system to generate a tailored compliance report.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Practices
prohibited.description: Article 5 prohibits certain AI practices entirely. If any apply, your system cannot be placed on the EU market.

gpai.title: General-Purpose AI
gpai.description: General-purpose AI models have specific obligations under Articles 51-55. Determine if your model qualifies and whether it poses systemic risk.

highRiskPathwayA.title: High-Risk: Product Safety (Annex I)
highRiskPathwayA.description: AI systems that are safety components of products covered by existing EU harmonisation legislation (Annex I) are high-risk under Article 6(1).

highRiskPathwayB.title: High-Risk: Annex III Stand-Alone Systems
highRiskPathwayB.description: AI systems used in the areas listed in Annex III are classified as high-risk under Article 6(2), unless they do not pose a significant risk of harm.

limitedRisk.title: Limited Risk: Transparency Obligations
limitedRisk.description: Certain AI systems carry transparency obligations under Article 50, regardless of other risk classifications.

questions.subliminalManipulation: Does your AI system deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness, or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective or effect of materially distorting behaviour and causing significant harm?
questions.exploitingVulnerabilities: Does your AI system exploit vulnerabilities of a specific group of persons due to their age, disability, or a specific social or economic situation, with the objective or effect of materially distorting their behaviour and causing significant harm?
questions.socialScoring: Does your AI system evaluate or classify natural persons based on their social behaviour or personal characteristics, leading to detrimental or unfavourable treatment in unrelated contexts or disproportionate to the behaviour?
questions.individualPredictivePolicing: Does your AI system make risk assessments of natural persons to predict the likelihood of a person committing a criminal offence, based solely on profiling or personality traits?
questions.untargetedFacialScraping: Does your AI system create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage?
questions.emotionInferenceWorkplaceEducation: Does your AI system infer emotions of natural persons in the areas of workplace or educational institutions, except where the system is intended to be put in place or on the market for medical or safety reasons?
questions.biometricCategorizationSensitive: Does your AI system categorize natural persons individually based on biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life, or sexual orientation?
questions.realtimeBiometricPublic: Does your AI system use real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes?
questions.isGpai: Is your AI model a general-purpose AI model, meaning it displays significant generality, is capable of competently performing a wide range of distinct tasks, and can be integrated into a variety of downstream systems or applications?
questions.gpaiFlops: Was your model trained using total compute greater than 10^25 FLOPs?
questions.isOpenSource: Is your model made available under a free and open-source licence that allows access, usage, modification, and distribution, and whose parameters, including weights, architecture, and usage information are made publicly available?
questions.designatedSystemicRisk: Has your model been designated by the European Commission as posing systemic risk based on its high-impact capabilities?
questions.annexIProduct: Is your AI system a safety component of a product, or is it itself a product, covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I (e.g., machinery, toys, medical devices, vehicles, aviation, rail, marine, lifts, pressure equipment)?
questions.annexIRequiresThirdParty: Does the relevant Annex I legislation require a third-party conformity assessment for the product to be placed on the market?
questions.annexIIIBiometrics: Is your AI system used for biometrics, including remote biometric identification, biometric categorization by sensitive attributes, or emotion recognition?
questions.annexIIICriticalInfrastructure: Is your AI system used as a safety component in the management and operation of critical infrastructure (road traffic, water, gas, heating, electricity)?
questions.annexIIIEducation: Is your AI system used in education or vocational training, for example determining access/admission, evaluating learning outcomes, determining educational levels, or monitoring student behaviour during tests?
questions.annexIIIEmployment: Is your AI system used in employment, workers management, or access to self-employment, for example for recruitment, screening applications, evaluating candidates, making decisions on employment terms, or monitoring/evaluating worker performance?
questions.annexIIIEssentialServices: Is your AI system used for access to or provision of essential private or public services (eligibility for public benefits, creditworthiness assessment, risk assessment for life/health insurance, or classifying emergency calls)?
questions.annexIIILawEnforcement: Is your AI system used in law enforcement, for example for individual risk assessment, as a polygraph, evaluating evidence reliability, or profiling persons during criminal investigations?
questions.annexIIIMigration: Is your AI system used in migration, asylum, or border control management, for example assessing irregular migration risk, examining asylum/visa/residence applications, or identifying persons at borders?
questions.annexIIIJusticeDemocracy: Is your AI system used in the administration of justice or democratic processes, for example assisting judicial authorities in researching/interpreting/applying law, or influencing the outcome of elections or referenda?
questions.interactsWithPersons: Does your AI system interact directly with natural persons (e.g., chatbot, voice assistant, virtual agent)?
questions.generatesSyntheticContent: Does your AI system generate or manipulate synthetic content such as images, audio, video, or text (including deepfakes)?
questions.emotionRecognition: Does your AI system perform emotion recognition (detecting or inferring emotions or intentions of natural persons based on biometric data)?
questions.biometricCategorizing: Does your AI system perform biometric categorization (classifying natural persons based on biometric data into groups based on specific attributes)?

entryIntoForce.title: EU AI Act enters into force
entryIntoForce.description: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 enters into force 20 days after publication in the Official Journal. Most provisions apply on a staggered timeline over the following 36 months.

prohibitionsApply.title: Prohibited practices apply
prohibitionsApply.description: Prohibitions on banned AI practices (Article 5) and AI literacy requirements (Article 4) become enforceable. Systems performing prohibited practices must cease operation in the EU.

gpaiRulesApply.title: GPAI rules apply
gpaiRulesApply.description: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models take effect, including documentation, copyright compliance, training data summaries, and additional obligations for models with systemic risk.

highRiskRequirements.title: High-risk requirements apply
highRiskRequirements.description: Full requirements for high-risk AI systems (Articles 6-15, 26-27) and transparency obligations (Article 50) become enforceable. Providers must meet risk management, data governance, documentation, record-keeping, transparency, human oversight, accuracy, and robustness requirements.

legacyGpai.title: Legacy GPAI and large-scale IT systems
legacyGpai.description: Obligations apply to GPAI models already on the market before August 2025, and to high-risk AI systems that are components of large-scale EU IT systems listed in Annex X.

productSafetyComponents.title: Product safety components
productSafetyComponents.description: Obligations for high-risk AI systems that are safety components of products covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation take full effect.

prohibited.stopDeployment: Stop deployment of the prohibited AI system in the EU immediately
prohibited.documentAndAssess: Document the prohibited system and assess alternatives that achieve the same goal without falling under prohibitions

highRisk.riskLifecycle: Establish a risk management system that operates throughout the entire lifecycle
highRisk.riskIdentify: Identify and analyze known and reasonably foreseeable risks
highRisk.riskEstimate: Estimate and evaluate risks from intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse
highRisk.riskPostMarket: Evaluate risks based on post-market monitoring data
highRisk.riskMeasures: Adopt risk management measures to eliminate or reduce risks
highRisk.riskTesting: Test the system to identify the most appropriate risk management measures
highRisk.riskCombinedEffects: Consider the combined effects when the system is used with other systems
highRisk.riskResidual: Document residual risks and communicate them to deployers
highRisk.dataGovernance: Ensure training, validation, and testing datasets are subject to appropriate data governance
highRisk.dataCollectionDocs: Document data collection processes and data sources
highRisk.dataRelevance: Assess data for relevance, representativeness, completeness, and statistical properties
highRisk.dataBiasExamination: Examine data for possible biases likely to affect health, safety, or fundamental rights
highRisk.dataGaps: Identify and address data gaps or shortcomings
highRisk.dataBiasMitigation: Apply appropriate measures for bias detection, correction, and mitigation
highRisk.transparencyInterpretable: Design the system to enable deployers to interpret output and use it appropriately
highRisk.oversightDesign: Design the system to allow effective oversight by natural persons during use
highRisk.accuracyRobustness: Ensure appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity

gpai.technicalDocs: Prepare and maintain technical documentation of the model
gpai.copyrightPolicy: Put in place a policy to comply with Union copyright law
gpai.trainingDataSummary: Draw up and make publicly available a sufficiently detailed summary of training data
gpai.downstreamInfo: Make information available to downstream providers to enable compliance

gpaiSystemic.modelEvaluation: Perform model evaluation including adversarial testing
gpaiSystemic.riskAssessment: Assess and mitigate possible systemic risks
gpaiSystemic.incidentTracking: Track, document, and report serious incidents to the AI Office
gpaiSystemic.cybersecurity: Ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity protection

limited.disclosureAI: Disclose that persons are interacting with an AI system
limited.labelSyntheticContent: Label AI-generated synthetic content in a machine-readable format
limited.informEmotionRecognition: Inform persons exposed to emotion recognition systems
limited.informBiometricCategorization: Inform persons exposed to biometric categorization systems
